perturbation.original_text: If the Purchaser or SHRA does not offer Ermilio an extension ("Extension") of his employment agreement, of even date herewith, with SHRA beyond the expiration date of the original one-year term ("Expiration Date"), then the Restricted Period shall be one (1) year from the date hereof. If the Purchaser or SHRA offers Ermilio an Extension, then the Restricted Period shall extend until the later of (i) two (2) years from the date hereof and (ii) one (1) year from the date of his termination of employment with the SHRA, or any Affiliate of SHRA, for any reason.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Purchaser or SHRA does not offer Ermilio an extension ("Extension") of his work agreement, of even date herewith, with SHRA beyond the expiration date of the original one-year term ("Expiration Date"), then the Restricted Period shall be one (1) year from the date hereof. If the Purchaser or SHRA offers Ermilio an Extension, then the Restricted Period shall extend until the later of (i) two (2) years from the date hereof and (ii) one (1) year from the date of his termination of work with the SHRA, or any Affiliate of SHRA, for any reason.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'employment agreement' with 'work agreement' and 'employment' with 'work'. This now makes it unclear about what an 'Extension' is reffering to in the 'work agreement' section. Is it regarding the 'employment agreement' or is it reffering to something else?
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Purchaser or SHRA does not offer Ermilio an extension ("Extension") of his work agreement, of even date herewith, with SHRA beyond the expiration date of the original one-year term ("Expiration Date"), then the Restricted Period shall be one (1) year from the date hereof. If the Purchaser or SHRA offers Ermilio an Extension, then the Restricted Period shall extend until the later of (i) two (2) years from the date hereof and (ii) one (1) year from the date of his termination of work with the SHRA, or any Affiliate of SHRA, for any reason.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'employment agreement' and 'employment'. The changed text replaces these with 'work agreement' and 'work'. The explanation states this change introduces ambiguity regarding what 'Extension' refers to in the context of the 'work agreement' section, making it unclear whether it pertains to the 'employment agreement' or something else. The contradicted text and changed text are in the same location, but the change introduced replaces 'employment agreement' with 'work agreement'. This creates a misalignment of terminology (Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction), leading to a potential contradiction because the definition of 'Extension' is based on agreement, and by changing the agreement type, it leads to uncertainty in enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: Ermilio agrees that during the Restricted Period, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Information and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of the Purchaser, any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Ermilio agrees that during the Confidentiality Term, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Data and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of the Purchaser, any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The terms “Restricted Period” is now changed to “Confidentiality Term”, while “Confidential Information” is changed to “Confidential Data”. This undermines the clarity since “Restricted Period” is defined later in Section 1, but “Confidentiality Term” is not. The section continues on using the term “Confidential Information”, instead of “Confidential Data”, creating confusion as to whether the terms are interchangeable, related, or even have the same meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ermilio agrees that during the Confidentiality Term, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Data and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of the Purchaser, any Confidential Information. At any time upon the Purchaser's request, Ermilio shall turn over to the Purchaser all books, notes, memoranda, manuals, notebooks, records and other documents made, compiled by, delivered to, or in the possession or control of Ermilio containing or concerning any Confidential Information, including all copies thereof, in any form or format, including any computer hard disks, wherever located, containing any such information, it being agreed that the same and all information contained therein are at all times the exclusive property of the Purchaser and its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "Restricted Period" to "Confidentiality Term" and "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data", while the contradicted text continues using "Confidential Information". This creates an in-text contradiction because the terms are used interchangeably without defining them or clarifying if they are related, leading to conflicting obligations and undermining clarity as described by the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, especially considering "Restricted Period" is defined later in Section 1, but “Confidentiality Term” is not. This makes the contract unclear and creates a strong, direct conflict. As the Confidentiality Term now refers to confidential data and the rest of the section refers to confidential information, there is now ambiguity as to which data and information the term refers to

perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to the Purchaser, its 2 <PAGE> Affiliates and/or those doing business with the Purchaser and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Ermilio in the course of his past, present and future dealings with the Purchaser and its Affiliates, which is not readily available to the public.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Details" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to the Purchaser, its 2 <PAGE> Affiliates and/or those doing business with the Purchaser and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Ermilio in the course of his past, present and future dealings with the Purchaser and its Affiliates, which is not readily available to the public.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is now changed to 'Confidential Details'. Section 2 uses “Confidential Information”, while later defining it as "Confidential Details", creating contradiction and confusion as to what the definition and section refers to. This creates ambiguity about the scope of the confidential information covered by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Details" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to the Purchaser, its 2 <PAGE> Affiliates and/or those doing business with the Purchaser and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Ermilio in the course of his past, present and future dealings with the Purchaser and its Affiliates, which is not readily available to the public. "Confidential Information" does not include any information, datum or fact: (a) currently available to the public as of the date hereof; (b) after it becomes available to the public other than as a result of a breach hereof or other wrongful conduct by Executive; (c) after it becomes available to Executive on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than the Company or its Affiliates or a person or entity breaching his or its confidentiality agreement or other relationship of confidence with the Company or its Affiliates; or (d) developed independently by Executive without any reference to or use whatsoever of any Confidential Information of the Company or its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information.' The changed text redefines it as 'Confidential Details'. The contradicted text uses both 'Confidential Details' and 'Confidential Information' in a way that implies they are distinct, while also appearing to define them in the same way. This creates a contradiction, as the agreement now inconsistently refers to two seemingly different terms (Details vs Information) that are purportedly the same, resulting in ambiguity as to the scope and meaning of each term within the context of the contract, which aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology causing In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the restrictive covenants contained in Section 1 and/or Section 2 of this Agreement shall be found by a court of competent jurisdiction to be unreasonable by reason of such restrictive covenants extending for too great a period of time or over too great a geographic area or by reason of such restrictive covenants being too extensive in any other respect, then such restrictive covenant shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make such restrictive covenant reasonable and enforceable under the circumstances.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the restrictive obligations contained in Section 1 and/or Section 2 of this Agreement shall be found by a court of competent jurisdiction to be unreasonable by reason of such restrictive obligations extending for too great a period of time or over too great a geographic area or by reason of such restrictive obligations being too extensive in any other respect, then such restrictive obligation shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make such restrictive obligation reasonable and enforceable under the circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the term “covenants” with “obligations” within the blue pencil doctrine section. By changing to 'obligations' it creates confusion. This is further amplified by continuing to refer to them as 'restrictive covenants' later in the text of the paragraph. What happens if an 'obligation' is deemed unenforceable; does it still apply to section 1 and 2?
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the restrictive obligations contained in Section 1 and/or Section 2 of this Agreement shall be found by a court of competent jurisdiction to be unreasonable by reason of such restrictive obligations extending for too great a period of time or over too great a geographic area or by reason of such restrictive obligations being too extensive in any other respect, then such restrictive obligation shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make such restrictive obligation reasonable and enforceable under the circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'covenants' to 'obligations' in the Changed Text, while retaining 'restrictive covenants' later in the same paragraph as well as in the Contradicted Text, creates ambiguity as to whether the enforceability determination applies equally to both covenants and obligations. This leads to a conflict because the original text consistently referred to 'restrictive covenants,' providing clarity. Now, the replaced term 'obligations' means that if an obligation is deemed unenforceable, it is unclear whether it still applies to sections 1 and 2 of the original agreement, creating a strong inconsistency and potential unenforceability issues, consistent with the definition of misaligned terminology.
